title: Argentina vs Algeria: World Cup 2026 Prediction | Squawka
original_text:
Our Argentina vs Algeria prediction lands on the reigning world champions opening their title defense in style on Tuesday, June 16 at 9:00 PM ET / 8:00 PM CT / 6:00 PM PT, with Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City the stage for the Group J curtain-raiser.
Argentina arrive as the team everyone else is chasing, and a first-up meeting with Algeria is the kind of test Lionel Scaloni’s side should pass without too much fuss. La Albiceleste have the deeper, more decorated squad, and a strong start matters in a 48-team format where group winners earn a kinder knockout path.
Algeria are no pushovers, though. Les Fennecs carry genuine attacking threat and will fancy their chances of springing a result if Argentina switch off. The gap in pedigree is wide, but a World Cup opener can be a tense, cagey affair before the favorite finds its rhythm.
bet365 prices Argentina at -239 to win, with Algeria a long shot at +600 and the draw at +350. The market is reflecting a clear mismatch on paper, and our read leans the same way. For the full futures picture, our World Cup 2026 outright betting odds page tracks where Argentina sit in the title race.
Market consensus: Kalshi traders price Argentina at 71%, the draw at 20%, and Algeria at 11%. Full breakdown below.

Group J — 2026 FIFA World Cup
The table below reflects the World Cup draw rather than any games played, with all four Group J sides yet to kick a ball. Argentina headline the group alongside Algeria, Austria, and Jordan.
TeamPosGPWDLGFGAGDPts
Argentina 1 0
Algeria 2 0
Austria 3 0
Jordan 4 0
Potential Match-Winners
Lionel Messi (Argentina): Probably the eight-time Ballon d’Or winner’s final World Cup. He needs no introduction and is just three goals short of the record World Cup goal tally (Miroslav Klose, 13).
Lautaro Martínez (Argentina): The Inter striker finished as Serie A’s top scorer with 17 goals in 30 games this season, and his movement gives Argentina a relentless focal point up top.
Julián Alvarez (Argentina): Alvarez added 8 goals and 4 assists in 29 La Liga outings for Atlético Madrid, and his versatility lets Scaloni pair him with Martínez or float him deeper.
Mohammed Amoura (Algeria): Amoura is Algeria’s sharpest outlet after 8 goals and 3 assists in 26 Bundesliga games for Wolfsburg, and his pace on the break is Les Fennecs’ best route back into the game.
Head-to-Head
These two have crossed paths just once at senior level, a high-scoring 2007 friendly at the Camp Nou that Argentina edged. There is no competitive history to lean on, so the form and squad gap matter more than any past meeting.
DateScoreCompetition
Jun 5, 2007 Argentina 4-3 Algeria Friendly
Form Coming Into the Tournament
Argentina reach the finals as the reigning world champions and topped a demanding South American qualifying group, a campaign built on the spine that lifted the trophy in 2022. With a settled core and elite depth in attack, La Albiceleste are among the shortest-priced sides to go deep again.
Algeria booked their place by winning their African qualifying group, ending a run that saw them miss the previous two World Cups. Les Fennecs lean on a talented European-based generation, and a strong showing here would mark a return to the global stage they have been chasing since 2014.
The pedigree gap is too wide to ignore, and we expect the champions to start their defense with a measured, controlled victory rather than a goal flurry. Our pick is Argentina to win at -239 with bet365, with a 2-0 scoreline the most likely route. New to the market? Our bet365 review walks through the sign-up. You can follow the rest of the group on the World Cup 2026 hub, see how this fits the wider slate via our soccer predictions, and read our Mexico vs South Africa preview for the opening night. Kick-off is 9:00 PM ET at Arrowhead Stadium, Kansas City.
Odds correct at the time of writing. Please gamble responsibly.
